intro.title: Mind Reading Check
intro.description: Question the assumptions you make about what others are thinking.

complete.title: Activity Complete
complete.message: By questioning your assumptions, you're building a more objective and compassionate mind.

step1.title: What happened?
step1.desc: Describe a recent moment where you felt judged or unsure.

step2.title: What went through your mind?
step2.desc: What did you assume they were thinking about you?

step3.title: Let's slow this down
step3.desc: Right now, this thought can feel true. But before accepting it as a fact, let's take a step back and look at it more closely.

step4.title: Is this true?
step4.desc: Is there any clear evidence (facts, not assumptions) that supports this thought?

step5.title: What else could be going on?

step6.title: Check your belief
step6.desc: How strongly do you believe your original thought now?

step7.title: A more balanced view
step7.desc: Write a thought that feels more fair and less certain.

step8.title: Next time
step8.desc: What could you do instead of assuming?

step9.title: Notice the shift
step9.desc: You explored different possibilities and created space for a more balanced view.

step10.title: A small shift
step10.desc: Your mind tries to fill in the gaps. But not every thought is a fact. Taking a step back helps you see things clearly.

common.completion_message: You've successfully completed this activity. Take a moment to appreciate your progress.
